title: Glassy-Jelly Egyptian Meringue Tart
description: Aromatic and romantic dessert featuring meringue nests, rose jelly, and orange blossom water.
instructions: Roll out puff pastry and place in a 9-inch tart pan with a removable bottom.
In a medium saucepan, combine rose petals, orange blossom water, granulated sugar, and salt. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Strain the rose petal mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean saucepan, discarding the solids.
In a small bowl, whisk together cornstarch and a splash of the rose petal mixture until smooth.
Return the rose petal mixture to the saucepan and whisk in the cornstarch mixture. Continue to cook over low heat, whisking constantly, until the mixture thickens, about 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir in the melted butter and rosemary leaves.
Pour the rose petal mixture into the prepared tart shell and smooth the top with an offset spatula.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the center is set.
While the tart is baking, prepare the meringue nests. In a clean bowl, whip the egg whites and granulated sugar until stiff peaks form.
Spoon the meringue onto the tart and smooth the top with an offset spatula.
Return the tart to the oven and bake for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until the meringue is golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes before serving.
